license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.yg2ftye
title: Lipid Biomarker Extraction and Elution into different Fractions from sediment
author: Lucia Leierer

steps_raw.instruction: Oven drying of sediment samples at 60 °C during 48:00:00 and subsampling to 5 g of homogenized sediment
Add 40 mL dichloromethane/methanol (DCM/MeOH) 9:1 v/v to the sediment
Mix for 00:30:00 in a sonicator (below 30 °C )
Transfer the solvent into a centrifuge tube and centrifugate for 00:10:00 at 4700 rpm
Filter centrifuged solvent through annealed glass wool and evaporate with N 2
Repeat step 2-5 two more times
Add small ball of anniled glass wool to column
Add 0.1 g of sand (50-70 mesh, previously fired at 450 °C during 10:00:00 ) to column
Add 1 g of silica (70-230 mesh, previously fired at 450 °C during 10:00:00 ) to column
Add lipids reconstituted in DCM with about 9 drops every 10 minutes
Leave the column covered in aluminium foil for at least 12 hours
Calculate dead volume (DV) using n-hexane
Elute n-alkanes with 3/8 DV using n-Hexane
Elute aromatics with 2 DV 8/2 v:v n-hexane/DCM
Elute ketones with 2 DV DCM
Elute alcohols with 2DV of 1/1 v:v DCM/Ethyl acetate
Elude acids and diols with 2 DV of Ethyl acetate
Elude other compounds with 2 DV of Methanol
Evaporate all fractions using N 2 at 30 °C
Store at -20 °C until measurement
Reconstitute n-alkanes with 5α-androstane (8mg/L) and 150 µL of DCM
Reconstitute aromatics with 5α-androstane (8mg/L) and 50 µL of DCM
Reconstitute ketones with 5α-androstan-3-ol (8mg/L) and 50 µL of DCM
Reconstitute acids and diols with Methyl C19:0 (8mg/L) and 40 µL of DCM and 10 µL of Ethyl acetate
Reconstitute alcohols with 5α-androstan-3-ol (8mg/L) and 40 µL of DCM and 10 µL of Ethyl acetate
Reconstitute other compounds with Methyl C19:0 (8mg/L) and 40 µL of DCM and 10 µL of Ethyl acetate
Add 100 µL of N,O-Bis(trimethylsilyl)trifluoroacetamide (BSTFA) + trimethylchlorosilane (TCMS) 99:1 v/v and 100 µL of pyridine to the extract
Derivatize at 80 °C for 01:00:00
Dry the sample under N 2
Derivatize fatty acids by adding 5 mL of MeOH, 400 µL of H 2 SO 4 to the extract
Heat at 70 °C for 04:00:00
Neutralize with saturated sodium bicarbonate solution
Extract three times with 3 mL n-hexane and dry under nitrogen
